title: atractocarpus fitzalanii subsp. tenuipes
intro: Atractocarpus fitzalanii subsp. tenuipes, also known as the 'Pandanus Lily', is a plant species found in Papua New Guinea and parts of Australia, characterized by its distinctive leaves and red berries. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Atractocarpus fitzalanii subsp. tenuipes include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a valuable resource for traditional medicine. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and skin conditions.
